steps.title: Identify the original angle and recall that dilation preserves angles
steps.content: From the diagram, $\angle A = 60°$ in triangle $ABC$. When a triangle is dilated by any scale factor, the resulting triangle is similar to the original, which means all angles are preserved. Only the side lengths change (multiplied by the scale factor of 5).

steps.title: Find angle X using angle correspondence
steps.content: Triangle $ABC$ is dilated to triangle $XYZ$. The vertices correspond as: $A \to X$, $B \to Y$, $C \to Z$. Since angles are preserved under dilation, $\angle X = \angle A = 60°$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
